response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack and web developer with a broad skill set, but he is fundamentally mismatched for this role. The job has a hard requirement for native-level Japanese language proficiency, which the candidate does not possess — his listed languages are English, Romanian, and basic German. Beyond the language disqualifier, his background is dominated by web, mobile, and cloud technologies (TypeScript, Node.js, Ember.js, MongoDB), with no demonstrated experience in embedded firmware, LAN-switch products, Layer-2/3 networking security (MACsec, 802.1X), Linux kernel development, or chip-vendor SDKs. His C experience is limited to a brief stint at National Instruments and a university thesis project, far from the depth required for security-critical embedded networking work.
strengths: Over 15 years of professional software development experience across diverse domains, demonstrating adaptability and technical breadth
Some C/C++ exposure at National Instruments and in embedded-adjacent projects (Linux, audio synthesis), providing a thin foundation to build on
Strong software craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development) that would be valuable in any engineering team
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency whatsoever — this is a non-negotiable hard requirement for the role
Zero experience in embedded networking security technologies: MACsec, IEEE 802.1X, Captive Portal, NETCONF, SNMP, or switch chip development
C programming is peripheral to his career; the role demands deep, primary-level C expertise for UNIX/Linux embedded firmware development

key_factors.factor: No embedded networking or security domain experience
